title: gaultheria erecta
intro: Gaultheria erecta, also known as Mountain Wintergreen, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains methyl salicylate, a compound similar to aspirin. Gaultheria erecta has been used to treat various health issues, including arthritis, sore muscles, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used topically or internally to harness its health benefits.
